title: Campus Improvement Plans
description: Campus Improvement Plans (CIPs) are official planning documents that outline each school's goals, strategies, and programs for improving student performance and overall campus operations. These plans affect every student, teacher, and staff member at a given school, as they guide decisions about instruction, resources, and support services. Parents and community members should be aware of these plans because they provide a transparent look at what each school is prioritizing and working toward in the coming school year, and state law requires that community input be part of the planning process.

timeline.summary: The board is asked to approve all 2025-2026 Campus Improvement Plans (CIPs) for each campus in the district. State law requires campus-level planning committees to annually assist principals in developing, reviewing, and revising CIPs. The board must annually review each CIP to ensure alignment with the District Improvement Plan and state goals under TEC Chapter 4. All CIPs were reviewed by the relevant Assistant Superintendent and deemed compliant. This is a routine annual approval, not a change to board policy text.
timeline.discussion: policy EM (teaching about controversial issues). Raised two specific concerns: (1) the addition of "unpatriotic or anti-American sentiments" as prohibited — terms he called undefined and ambiguous; (2) a new "prohibited terms" section banning "Latinx" and "minor attracted person," which he opposed. He urged the board to give these revisions appropriate scrutiny.
timeline.notes: References Board Policy BQ and BQB (Legal) as authority. Not a policy text amendment — this is the annual statutory approval of campus-level plans. Each CIP was reviewed by the supervising Assistant Superintendent. Submitted by Dr. Ted Landry, Deputy Superintendent. Page 55 (TEA Approver Access for Dr. Ted Landry) does not reference a TASB policy code and concerns an administrative access request, not a board policy change.
